title: Harness Self-Healing KB — hub knowledge base of error patterns and confirmed resolutions
what: Hub maintains WAI-Spoke/hub/kb/ — a structured knowledge base of observed error patterns, confirmed resolutions, and the sessions that verified each fix. When Ozi (on any node) encounters an error, it matches the error text and context against KB patterns. If a match is found with auto_apply: true, the resolution is applied automatically. If auto_apply: false, the confirmed resolution steps are surfaced to the user. Errors with no match create KB candidates (status: unresolved) for future promotion. Confirmed entries that apply to all spokes are nominated as teaching candidates, feeding the fleet-wide self-improvement pipeline.
why: Self-healing eliminates recurring manual interventions. Today the same error — a mismatched hub_path, a stale session-guard lock, a missing WAI-State field — appears on multiple spokes across multiple sessions. Each time, a human resolves it from scratch. The KB captures verified resolutions once, applies them automatically where safe, and surfaces them confidently where human confirmation is required. Over time the system heals itself faster with each iteration.

use_cases.title: Known error matched and auto-applied
use_cases.trigger: Ozi encounters an error during operation; error text matches a KB entry with auto_apply: true
use_cases.outcome: Resolution steps executed automatically; operation retried; outcome logged to operational trace with kb_entry_id reference; confirmed_by updated if resolution succeeded

use_cases.title: Known error matched, surfaced to user
use_cases.trigger: Ozi encounters an error matching a KB entry with auto_apply: false
use_cases.outcome: Ozi surfaces resolution steps to user with context: 'This matches a known issue (kb-entry-id). Confirmed steps: [...]'; user confirms; Ozi applies steps; confirms success back to KB entry

use_cases.title: New error creates KB candidate
use_cases.trigger: Ozi encounters an error with no matching KB pattern
use_cases.outcome: KB candidate written to hub/WAI-Spoke/hub/kb/candidates/{error_hash}.json with status: unresolved; Navigator alerted if same unresolved candidate recurs across 3 fleet sessions

use_cases.title: Fleet-wide KB entry nominated as teaching
use_cases.trigger: KB entry reaches confirmed_by status and applies_to: all
use_cases.outcome: Teaching candidate written to generate-teaching/candidates/ with source: kb_resolution; same peer review quality gate as any other teaching; no fast-track

patterns.name: Match-before-escalate
patterns.description: Every error Ozi encounters must be matched against the KB before any escalation to the user. If a match exists, the KB-guided path is always preferred over ad-hoc resolution. This prevents regression — the same problem should never require a fresh human decision after a confirmed resolution exists.

patterns.name: Confirmed-before-auto-apply safety gate
patterns.description: auto_apply: true is never set at KB candidate creation. It requires an explicit confirmed_by field (a session_id where the resolution was successfully applied), confirmed_at timestamp, and a human-readable confirmation note. This gate ensures automated resolutions are verified before they run unattended.

patterns.name: KB-to-teaching pipeline
patterns.description: The KB is a staging area for teachings. Resolutions that prove fleet-wide (applies_to: all, confirmed across multiple sessions) graduate to the teaching pipeline. KB entries that remain spoke-specific (applies_to: spoke_type or local-only) stay as reference entries and do not generate teachings.

patterns.name: Local KB vs Hub KB authority hierarchy
patterns.description: Each node maintains a local KB for spoke-specific patterns. Hub KB is the fleet-wide authoritative source. On migration, spoke KB is merged with hub KB; hub entries win on conflict. Local-only entries remain in local KB but are flagged for review during migration — they may be candidates for hub promotion if patterns are general.

how.trigger: Any Ozi error during operation — hook failure, path mismatch, session-guard conflict, missing WAI-State field, schema validation failure, or any exception with a parseable error string
steps_summary: 1. Ozi captures error text and context (operation type, file being accessed, hook name, lug id if applicable)

2. Ozi reads hub/WAI-Spoke/hub/kb/entries/ + local WAI-Spoke/kb/entries/ (local checked first, then hub); pattern match against error text using entry.pattern (regex); also checks context_clues[] for structural match
3a. Match found, auto_apply: true — Ozi executes resolution_steps[] in order; logs outcome to operational trace with kb_entry_id; if resolution succeeds, operation retried; result logged

3b. Match found, auto_apply: false — Ozi surfaces entry to user: 'Known issue (id): [description]. Confirmed steps: [resolution_steps[]]'; user confirms; Ozi executes steps; success updates confirmed_by if not yet set
3c. No match — Ozi writes KB candidate to hub/WAI-Spoke/hub/kb/candidates/{error_slug}-{date}.json with status: unresolved, raw error text, context snapshot, observed_at, session_id
4. Navigator (hub) monitors candidates/; if same error_slug appears in 3 separate session candidates within a version window, Navigator alert raised with concern: recurring_unresolved_pattern
5. When user or hub Ozi resolves a candidate, entry promoted: status promoted to confirmed, confirmed_by set, confirmed_at set, moved to entries/
6. Confirmed entry with applies_to: all triggers teaching nomination: candidate written to generate-teaching/candidates/ with source: kb_resolution, entry_id reference
constraints: KB pattern match is read-only — matching never modifies an entry; only resolution application and user confirmation trigger writes
auto_apply may only be set to true by an explicit hub Ozi review action, never at candidate creation
Hub KB entries win over local KB entries on conflict (same pattern match, different resolution); local entry flagged as superseded
KB candidates older than 30 days without promotion are flagged as stale; Navigator includes stale count in fleet health briefing
Teaching nomination from KB requires applies_to: all — spoke-specific entries never nominate teachings

triggers: Any Ozi error during operation (hooks, lug execution, migration steps, wakeup checks)
KB candidate promotion by user or hub Ozi after resolution
Consolidation event — hub Ozi reviews new candidates and promotes qualified entries
not_when: KB is not consulted for expected control-flow conditions (lug not found, empty queue) — only genuine errors trigger KB lookup

KB match does not override explicit user instruction — if user provides an ad-hoc resolution during a session, that takes precedence; the session's outcome may be used to update the KB entry afterward
when.frequency: KB lookup: every Ozi error (runtime, low latency). KB promotion: on resolution confirmation (session-level). Teaching nomination: on confirmed entry reaching applies_to: all (event-driven).

inputs.name: kb_entry
inputs.description: A confirmed KB entry; lives at hub/WAI-Spoke/hub/kb/entries/{id}.json and at local WAI-Spoke/kb/entries/{id}.json for spoke-specific patterns

schema.pattern: string — regex pattern matched against error text; or structural_match for non-text errors
context_clues: string — additional structural signals to confirm match (file accessed, hook name, operation type)
resolution_steps: string — ordered steps to resolve; written for agent execution
schema.confirmed_by: string | null — session_id where resolution was successfully applied; null until confirmed
schema.applies_to: enum: all | spoke_type:<type> | local — scope of applicability
schema.auto_apply: boolean — if true, steps applied automatically without user confirmation; requires confirmed_by to be set first

seed_entry.pattern: hub_path in WAI-State.json points to .* but
resolution_steps: Verify actual hub repo path: ls ~/projects/wheelwright/hub
Update WAI-State.json hub_path field to match verified path
Re-run session init to confirm resolution

inputs.name: kb_candidate
inputs.description: Unresolved error that had no KB match; lives at hub/WAI-Spoke/hub/kb/candidates/{error_slug}-{date}.json pending promotion

schema.id: string — generated slug from error text
schema.occurrence_count: integer — incremented when same error_slug appears in subsequent sessions

outputs.name: resolution_outcome
outputs.description: Appended to PathGraph operational trace on each KB match + resolution attempt

schema.delta: string | null — what was different from expected if resolution partially succeeded

outputs.name: teaching_candidate
outputs.description: Written to generate-teaching/candidates/ when a KB entry with applies_to: all reaches confirmed status

schema.entry_id: string — kb entry that generated this candidate
schema.title: string — generalized title for the teaching

state_changes: kb/entries/{id}.json: confirmed_by and confirmed_at set when resolution verified in session
kb/candidates/{slug}.json: occurrence_count incremented on each recurrence; status set to promoted when entry moves to entries/
harness-state.json: kb_entry_count and kb_candidate_count updated on each promotion
generate-teaching/candidates/: new file created on teaching nomination from KB entry

constraints: auto_apply may never be set at candidate creation; requires explicit confirmed_by before it can be set true
Hub KB entries supersede local KB entries on conflict; local entry flagged as superseded but not deleted
KB lookup must complete in under 500ms; entries are indexed by pattern prefix for fast regex scan
KB candidates not promoted within 30 days are marked stale; Navigator includes stale candidate count in fleet health briefing
Teaching nominations from KB require applies_to: all; spoke-type or local entries cannot nominate teachings
On migration: spoke local KB entries are merged into migration lug completion record and delivered to hub for review; hub decides which to promote to hub KB

tests.description: An error matching a KB entry with auto_apply: true and confirmed_by set results in resolution steps executed automatically; operational trace contains kb_entry_id; no user prompt issued
tests.pass_criteria: PathGraph trace entry written with op_type: kb_resolution, auto_applied: true, resolution_succeeded: true; no escalation to user

tests.description: An error matching a KB entry with auto_apply: false surfaces the resolution steps to user; Ozi message includes entry id and resolution_steps[]
tests.pass_criteria: Ozi output includes KB entry id and all resolution_steps in order; user confirmation triggers step execution

tests.description: An error with no KB match results in a new candidate file written to kb/candidates/; candidate has status: unresolved, raw_error, context_snapshot, observed_at, session_id
tests.pass_criteria: Candidate file present after error; all required fields present; status = unresolved

tests.description: A KB entry with applies_to: all that reaches confirmed_by status triggers creation of a teaching candidate in generate-teaching/candidates/
tests.pass_criteria: Candidate file present in generate-teaching/candidates/ with source: kb_resolution and correct entry_id reference

tests.description: When a spoke local KB entry matches the same pattern as a hub KB entry (same id), the hub entry is used for resolution; local entry is marked superseded
tests.pass_criteria: Resolution uses hub entry steps; local entry file contains superseded_by field pointing to hub entry id

tests.description: Same unresolved candidate error_slug appearing in 3 separate session candidates triggers Navigator alert with concern: recurring_unresolved_pattern
tests.pass_criteria: Navigator alert written after third occurrence; alert references candidate slug and occurrence_count

health.verification_note: Design spec authored from plan file /home/mario/.claude/plans/how-can-our-design-warm-raven.md Loop 4 (4A and 4B). Seed entry kb-hub-path-mismatch-v1 included as first concrete example.

perceive: Read this spec (spec-harness-kb-v1). Read related spec spec-harness-self-maintaining-wheel-v1 for structural parity context. Read spec-harness-operational-traces-v1 for PathGraph trace schema. IMPORTANT: an existing KB structure lives at hub/learnings/kb/ with a different schema — read hub/learnings/kb-index.json to understand its format before implementing. Determine whether existing entries should be migrated to the new schema or whether hub/learnings/kb/ and hub/WAI-Spoke/hub/kb/ serve different purposes (learnings/kb = human reference library; WAI-Spoke/hub/kb/ = Ozi-queryable error resolution entries). The two can coexist. hub/WAI-Spoke/hub/kb/ does not exist yet — must be created as new. Confirm harness-state.json has kb_entry_count and kb_candidate_count fields (add if absent).
execute: Implement the KB loop in three phases: (1) KB structure — create hub/WAI-Spoke/hub/kb/entries/ and candidates/ directories; write seed entry kb-hub-path-mismatch-v1. (2) KB lookup in Ozi — on any error, run pattern match against entries/ before escalation; implement auto_apply and surface-to-user paths. (3) Candidate lifecycle — implement candidate creation on no-match; promotion flow on resolution; Navigator alert on 3+ occurrences. (4) Teaching nomination — implement applies_to: all check on confirmation; write candidate to generate-teaching/candidates/. (5) Migration merge — add local KB export step to migration lug template.
verify: All six tests in tests[] pass. Seed entry kb-hub-path-mismatch-v1 present in hub/WAI-Spoke/hub/kb/entries/ after implementation. A simulated error matching the seed entry pattern surfaces resolution steps to user without triggering auto_apply (auto_apply: false on seed). KB candidate created for a novel error not matching any entry. Teaching nomination fires correctly on a test entry promoted to applies_to: all + confirmed_by set.
